chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, CIRCUIT COURT HYDERABAD Cr. Bail Application No.S-83 of 2022 Cr. Bail Application No.S-84 of 2022 Cr. Bail Application No.S-85 of 2022 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE(S) 29.08.2022. Applicant is present on ad-interim pre-arrest bail. Mr. Altaf Sachal Awan, Advocate for applicant. Mr. Zahid Ali Khoso, Advocate for complainant. Mr. Imran Ali Abbasi, Assistant P.G. O R D E R MUHAMMAD IQBAL KALHORO, J- Applicant and co-accused allegedly assaulted on complainant over some dispute on sale of a car and inflicted three injuries on his person near Railway Mori, Jamshoro on 15.11.2021 at 04:45 p.m. and took his car. Out of them one injury 337-A(ii) PPC is non-bailalbe, punishable upto five years, whereas the remaining two injuries 337-A(i) PPC are baialble. But none of them is attributed to applicant. It is informed that entire case has proceeded except I.O who is likely to be examined on the next date of hearing i.e. 09.09.2022. In view of the ratio laid down by the Hon’ble Supreme Court in the case of REHMATULLAH v. The STATE (2011 SCMR 1332) that when the case has been reached advance stage, the ad-interim pre-arrest bail granted to the accused cannot be recalled and it shall be left to the trial Court to decide the case through full and final judgment which in this case is likely to be announced in a period of one month. 2. In view of such situation and no specific role of applicant, with the consent of the parties, these applications are allowed and the ad-interim pre-arrest bail of the applicants is hereby confirmed with direction to the learned trial Court to examine the I.O on the next date of hearing without fail and announce the judgment within a period of one month thereof.

The STATE (2011 SCMR 1332) that when the case has been reached advance stage, the ad-interim pre-arrest bail granted to the accused cannot be recalled and it shall be left to the trial Court to decide the case through full and final judgment which in this case is likely to be announced in a period of one month. 2. In view of such situation and no specific role of applicant, with the consent of the parties, these applications are allowed and the ad-interim pre-arrest bail of the applicants is hereby confirmed with direction to the learned trial Court to examine the I.O on the next date of hearing without fail and announce the judgment within a period of one month thereof. No party shall be allowed adjournment on any ground. 3. With the above observations, the applications are disposed of accordingly. JUDGE Shahid

With the above observations, the applications are disposed of accordingly. JUDGE Shahid ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, CIRCUIT COURT HYDERABAD Cr. Bail Application No.S-459 of 2022 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE(S) 1. For orders on office objections. 2. For hearing of main case. 29.08.2022. Applicant is present on ad-interim pre-arrest bail. Mr. Badaruddin Khoso, Advocate for applicant. Ms. Sana Memon, Assistant P.G. = Learned Counsel for the applicant submits that he has made a compromise with the complainant party and application in this regard has already been filed which is fixed tomorrow before the trial Court for orders. Adjourned to 02.09.2022. JUDGE Shahid

Adjourned to 02.09.2022. JUDGE Shahid ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, CIRCUIT COURT HYDERABAD Cr. Bail Application No.S-436 of 2022 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE(S) 1. For orders on office objections. 2. For hearing of main case. 29.08.2022. Applicants are present on ad-interim pre-arrest bail. Mr. Raja Hans Raj Naurang, Advocate for applicant. Ms. Sana Memon, Assistant P.G. = Mr. Muhammad Aleem Arain, Advocate holds brief on behalf of Mr. Abdul Hameed Bajwa, Advocate for complainant, and seeks time. As a last chance, adjourned to 12.09.2022. JUDGE Shahid

As a last chance, adjourned to 12.09.2022. JUDGE Shahid ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, CIRCUIT COURT HYDERABAD Cr. Bail Application No.S-405 of 2022 Cr. Bail Application No.S-647 of 2022 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE(S) 29.08.2022. Mr. Ghulamullah Chang, Advocate for applicants alongwith applicant Lechman. Mr. Sajjad Ali Gopang, Advocate for complainant. Ms. Sana Memon, Assistant P.G. = I have heard the parties at some length. It has been pointed out that bail application of the applicants was dismissed by the trial Court during investigation before submission of the challan and the medical reports simply on the basis of 161 Cr.P.C statements of the witnesses. In these circumstances, with the consent of all the parties, these bail applications are disposed of and applicants are put on liberty to file a fresh bail application before the trial Court. Meanwhile, the challan shall be submitted with all the relevant papers including medical reports before the trial Court for consideration afresh without being influenced by the impugned orders within 10(ten) days after submission of the application. Applicant Lechman S/o Heero is on ad-interim pre-arrest bail in Criminal Bail Application No.S-405 of 2022 which application is hereby converted into an application for protective bail and applicant Lechman is granted protective bail for a period of 08(eight) days from today without touching merits of the case against the surety already deposited by him vide order dated 06.06.2022. JUDGE Shahid
